testcases.input: vals = [1,3,2,1,3], edges = [[0,1],[0,2],[2,3],[2,4]] Output: 6 Explanation: There are 5 good paths consisting of a single node. There is 1 additional good path: 1 -> 0 -> 2 -> 4. (The reverse path 4 -> 2 -> 0 -> 1 is treated as the same as 1 -> 0 -> 2 -> 4.) Note that 0 -> 2 -> 3 is not a good path because vals[2] > vals[0].
testcases.expectedOutput: 6 Explanation: There are 5 good paths consisting of a single node. There is 1 additional good path: 1 -> 0 -> 2 -> 4. (The reverse path 4 -> 2 -> 0 -> 1 is treated as the same as 1 -> 0 -> 2 -> 4.) Note that 0 -> 2 -> 3 is not a good path because vals[2] > vals[0].

testcases.input: vals = [1,1,2,2,3], edges = [[0,1],[1,2],[2,3],[2,4]] Output: 7 Explanation: There are 5 good paths consisting of a single node. There are 2 additional good paths: 0 -> 1 and 2 -> 3.
testcases.expectedOutput: 7 Explanation: There are 5 good paths consisting of a single node. There are 2 additional good paths: 0 -> 1 and 2 -> 3.
